value_picks.name: Abbott Laboratories
value_picks.why_undervalued: Shares were pressing recent 52-week lows in late March 2026 while the market digested slower post-pandemic diagnostics growth, despite Abbott’s diversified med-tech and nutrition portfolio and solid balance sheet.
value_picks.bull_case: Normalization in diagnostics plus steady device growth (Libre, structural heart) can re-accelerate earnings, with buybacks and dividend supporting total return as valuation re-rates toward historical averages.

value_picks.name: Stryker Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: Stryker traded near its 52-week lows into March 2026 amid procedure-volume and rate/valuation compression fears, even as it continued to generate strong free cash flow and hold leading positions in orthopedics and surgical tech.
value_picks.bull_case: If elective procedure volumes stay resilient and margin initiatives persist, Stryker’s earnings power and FCF can compound, supporting a rebound from a de-rated multiple.

value_picks.name: Unilever PLC ADR
value_picks.why_undervalued: Unilever was appearing on recent 52-week-low screens into late March 2026, reflecting investor skepticism about volume growth and FX, while the business still produces dependable cash flow from global household/personal care brands.
value_picks.bull_case: Improving mix/pricing discipline and portfolio focus can lift margins and cash conversion, and a high FCF yield supports buybacks/dividends while sentiment mean-reverts.

value_picks.name: The Progressive Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: Progressive showed up on 52-week-low lists despite strong underwriting execution; investors worried about premium cycle normalization after rapid pricing actions.
value_picks.bull_case: Sustained discipline on loss costs and continued growth in net premiums written can keep ROE high; even a modest multiple expansion from a low-teens P/E can drive upside.

value_picks.name: Boston Scientific Corporation
value_picks.why_undervalued: Boston Scientific was near 52-week lows as investors rotated away from higher-multiple med-tech, despite durable demand in cardiovascular/urology franchises and consistent execution.
value_picks.bull_case: Pipeline wins and continued share gains can sustain mid-teens EPS growth; a less hostile rate/valuation backdrop could support multiple recovery.

momentum_picks.name: AN2 Therapeutics, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Small-cap biotech surged through March 2026 as investors chased clinical/program updates and high beta healthcare momentum after a sharp drawdown earlier in the year.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: High volatility and binary clinical/newsflow risk; position sizing is critical.

momentum_picks.name: Tilly's, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Shares exploded higher after a fiscal Q4 earnings beat and improving comparable sales/margins, triggering short covering in a low-float retailer.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Liquidity/volatility is extreme; any demand softness could reverse gains quickly.

momentum_picks.name: Day One Biopharmaceuticals, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Commercial uptake expectations and upcoming clinical catalysts helped DAWN break out as biotech risk appetite returned in early 2026.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Execution on launch and trial timelines drives outcome; downside can be large if expectations reset.

momentum_picks.name: Apellis Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Apellis rallied sharply on improving sentiment around core franchise durability and anticipation of quarterly results, fueling strong 1–3 month momentum.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Headline risk around guidance/reimbursement remains; momentum could fade on any disappointment.

momentum_picks.name: Tango Therapeutics, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: TNGX jumped after newsflow around collaborations/updates and financial results, amplifying biotech momentum through March 2026.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Partnership and pipeline updates can drive sharp swings; risk is binary around clinical progress.

watchlist_updates.detail: S&P 500 declined 1.9% for week amid Middle East conflict and Fed rate hold.
Russell 2000 down amid broad market weakness.
Nasdaq down 2.1% as oil swings above $110.
FedEx up $26 premarket on solid earnings and guidance.
Planet Labs surged 26% after Q4 breakeven EPS and 41% revenue growth beating estimates.
HSBC upgraded to buy PT $215, less reliant on Middle East oil.
Exxon down 2% session but strong YTD on oil rally.
